texto: The winds of potential change blow constantly through our existence altering potentialities until a tipping point or nexus shakes our thread into a different weave, a new existence. It is our pattern-sensing consciousnesses that tricks us into believing remaining static is an option, that this day is like the next or the one before, as if the chaos that change will inevitably bring can be avoided. It's a comforting lie . . .
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Larry J. Dunlap: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “The winds of potential change blow constantly through our existence altering potentialities until…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, change, life-philosophy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e life-philosophy.

texto: Maybe it shouldn’t have come as such a surprise, but like a tooth extraction, there was a moment of intense pain and then a numbing sense of loss. I was officially unmarried.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Larry J. Dunlap: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Maybe it shouldn’t have come as such a surprise, but like a tooth extraction, there was a moment …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, divorce, emotional-pain pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e emotional-pain.

texto: We humans desperately seek stability in hopes, I think, that we can control our lives, though that isn't the way things work. Everything is in flux; we are dynamic beings born with expiration dates into an uncertain Universe.
explicacao: Citação de Larry J. Dunlap sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “We humans desperately seek stability in hopes, I think, that we can control our lives, though tha…” articula reflexao, human-nature, mortality em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e mortality.
